purpose: Classify the known whole-file UBS signal in the large extension runtime modules so future reviews can separate guarded or test-only baseline noise from new production defects.

rust_module_no_cargo_summary.note: The Rust module JSON category inventory preserves all critical and warning counts. Info rows are advisory and differ by two between the meta-runner and direct module path.

changed_line_gate.policy: Critical or warning UBS findings on staged added or modified Rust lines remain blocking. This baseline only classifies whole-file findings that UBS reports on unchanged lines in the two large runtime modules.

finding_categories.title: Potential panics via unwrap/expect
finding_categories.review_note: Samples are dominated by test helpers and explicit invariant checks in the WASM and extension conformance suites. Treat new production unwrap/expect additions in these files as actionable.

finding_categories.title: panic! macro(s) present
finding_categories.review_note: Sample locations are in test modules that intentionally panic when an async worker must surface a test failure to the harness.

finding_categories.title: unreachable! may panic if reached
finding_categories.review_note: Current samples are expectation guards in extension hostcall tests. New production unreachable! remains actionable.

finding_categories.title: Rc<RefCell<..>> borrow panics possible
finding_categories.review_note: QuickJS and WASM bridge state is intentionally single-threaded. New shared-runtime RefCell use still needs review.

finding_categories.title: Poisoned lock handling via unwrap/expect
finding_categories.review_note: Whole-file lock findings are retained as review inventory; changed-line additions continue to fail the delta gate.

finding_categories.title: thread::sleep in async
finding_categories.review_note: Current samples are inside blocking worker threads spawned to drain extension exec streams, not executor-thread sleeps.

finding_categories.title: std::thread::spawn inside async fn
finding_categories.review_note: Current samples are deliberate blocking bridge workers for process I/O and QuickJS runtime isolation. New spawns need explicit lifecycle review.

finding_categories.title: clone() inside loops - potential perf hit
finding_categories.review_note: Perf noise is tracked separately from safety findings. Treat hot-path additions as optimization work, not as a blocker for unrelated changed lines.

finding_categories.title: Security token generated with non-cryptographic randomness
finding_categories.review_note: The sampled line is Instant::now() measuring warm-reset latency, not token, nonce, salt, key, or signature generation.

finding_categories.title: Secret, signature, or token compared with ==/!=
finding_categories.review_note: Samples include path normalization, ASCII escaping, and structural comparisons rather than secret material. New comparisons involving credentials remain actionable.

finding_categories.title: Command executable from untrusted-looking value
finding_categories.review_note: The two Command::new(&cmd) sinks are reached after exec capability policy and evaluate_exec_mediation() classify or deny dangerous commands before dispatch_hostcall_exec_ref().

finding_categories.title: Path join/push with untrusted-looking segment
finding_categories.review_note: Samples are source enumeration and extension entry discovery paths that resolve through safe_canonicalize/entry filtering. New filesystem writes or extraction paths remain actionable.

finding_categories.title: Archive entry path traversal risk
finding_categories.review_note: Samples are recursive directory enumeration vectors, not archive extraction or unpacking. New archive handling must use enclosed-name or containment checks.

finding_categories.title: assert! macros present (panic surface)
finding_categories.review_note: The largest cluster is from #[cfg(test)] conformance and WASM tests. Runtime assertions outside tests should be converted to explicit errors.

finding_categories.title: Direct indexing/slicing may panic
finding_categories.review_note: Representative WASM memory indexing is preceded by checked_memory_range/caller_read_bytes. New unchecked indexing from external offsets remains actionable.

finding_categories.title: serde/toml deserialization unwrap/expect
finding_categories.review_note: Samples are round-trip serialization tests for host stream chunks, runtime risk models, and quota configs.

finding_categories.title: String allocation inside loop
finding_categories.review_note: Tracked as performance inventory for future focused optimization work; changed-line additions remain visible.
